claim: Prediction in atom ATOM_T001_S040_PRE_01 ("The one we're going to just plot here is going to be whether the language mod...") holds if and only if the first-principle constraint in atom ATOM_T013_S025_FIR_01 ("But we are not losing the sight of the fact that it is in principle possible ...") is binding.
first_principles_validity_hypothesis: The prediction "The one we're going to just plot here is going to be whet..." is forced by the underlying constraint "But we are not losing the sight of the fact that it is in..."; if the constraint is not binding the prediction may still hold by accident but the binding case is the load-bearing version of this candidate.
why_novel_vs_speaker: The speaker introduces the prediction (snippet S040) and the first-principle (snippet S025) in separate contexts; the speaker does not state that the second forces the first.
why_potentially_useful: A prediction that is forced by a first-principle is more falsifiable than a free-floating prediction: if the principle holds and the prediction fails, one of them is wrong, which is a binary research question.
